feed.title: garden.errbufferoverfl.me - home
feed.description: An open collection of notes, resources, sketches, and explorations I'm cultivating.
feed.subtitle: Recent content in home on
feed.author: errbuffoverfl
feed.rights: This content is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International unless otherwise specified.

entry.title: intercropping
entry.summary: Because there is some degree of ambiguity about what a [[notes/polyculture|polyculture]] this can have a flow on effect to associated terms such as intercropping.

entry.title: How to Stand Up to a Dictator
entry.summary: What will you sacrifice for the truth? Maria Ressa has spent decades speaking truth to power. But her work tracking disinformation networks seeded by her own government, spreading lies to its own citizens laced with anger and hate, has landed her in trouble with the most powerful man in the country: President Duterte.

entry.title: rugged individualism
entry.summary: A term coined by US-Republican president Herbert Hoover, it’s a term that indicates that an individual is self-reliant and independent from outside, usually state assistance.

entry.title: Making Plastic Yarn (Plarn)
entry.summary: Step 1 Flatten the bad so the sides are folded out and the sides are straight forming a rectangle. Trim the bag, removing the top of the bag where the handles are, and the bottom above the seam.

entry.title: Fat Worm Recipe
entry.summary: 50% Chicken Layer Pellets 10% Wheat or Cornflour 10% Powdered Whole Milk/Skim Milk 20% Bran or Wheat Meal 10% Agricultural lime or dolomite Mix the ingredients and sprinkle lightly on the food scraps about once a week.

entry.title: The Engineering Anthology
entry.summary: %% Begin Waypoint %% %% End Waypoint %%

entry.title: tools
entry.summary: %% Begin Waypoint %% cytoscapejs distributed-press dopely npm-ses temporal %% End Waypoint %%

entry.title: Languages that implement object capabilities
entry.summary: The following programming languages implement an [[object-capability-model|Object-capability model]]: Act 1 (1981) Eden (1985) Emerald (1987) Trusty Scheme (1992) W7 (1995) Joule (1996) Original-E (1997) Oz-E (2005) Joe-E (2005) CaPerl (2006) Emily (2006) Caja (2007–2021) Monte (2008–present) Pony (2014–present) Wyvern (2012–present) Newspeak (2007–present) Hacklang (2021-present) Rholang (2018-present)

entry.title: Object-capability model
entry.summary: The object-capability model (also seen written as “ocap”) is a [[object-capability security]] concept that describes a computer security model. In ocap a capability describes a transferable right to perform one or more operations on a given object.

entry.title: Intercropping Examples
entry.summary: The following examples were collated from online searches of intercropping online, and many were rightly or wrongly compiled on the foundation of companion planting.

entry.title: eutrophication
entry.summary: The process by which an entire body of water, or parts of it become enriched with minerals and nutrients, particularly nitrogen and phosphorus.

entry.title: Polyculture Practices
entry.summary: While there are no hard or fast rules to using a [[notes/polyculture|Polyculture]] there are common practices that can be implemented depending on the plants, spatial distribution and time spent cohabiting.

entry.title: polyculture
entry.summary: A polyculture is the practice of growing more than one crop or species in the same space at the same time.

entry.title: bookmarks
entry.summary: %% Begin Waypoint %% 5-types-of-threat-how-those-who-want-to-divide-us-use-language-to-stoke-violence 17-forbidden-places-you-cant-visit a-dangerous-pesticide-isnt-being-monitored-in-key-bird-of-prey-populations-were-shedding-light-on-that-gap a-list-of-programming-playgrounds a-massachusetts-bill-could-allow-prisoners-to-swap-their-organs-for-their-freedom a-racially-biased-scoring-system-helps-pick-who-receives-housing a-small-mistake-does-not-a-complex-systems-failure-make a-story-of-legends-families-and-capitalism-a-candid-history-of-the-christmas-tree abolition-ecology-and-afrofuturism-some-thoughts adjusting-the-intensity-of-farming-can-help-address-climate-change against-a-liberal-abolitionism against-self-work-a-critique-of-care-as-a-form-of-labor all-my-takeout-has-delivered-a-mountain-of-trash-so-i-asked-experts-how-to-minimize-it an-allys-guide-to-using-the-right-words-for-the-sex-industry anarchism-and-cryptocurrency anatomy-of-three-leaked-us-intelligence-documents andrew-tate-research-has-long-shown-how-feminist-progress-is-always-followed-by-a-misogynistic-backlash anti-racism-and-anti-colonialism-an-open-letter-to-my-black-kin appropriate-technology-permacomputing-post-growth-and-ecomodernism april-king-refresh-vs.

entry.title: Using Pytest with Flask
entry.summary: What to test? Unit tests should focus on testing a small unit of code in isolation: Database models Utility functions Functional tests should focus on how the view functions operate:

entry.title: NASA's ADLR Framework for Lessons Learned
entry.summary: Example: NASA Lesson Number 5756 The ADLR framework is broken up into four parts: Abstract: a two or three sentence summary of what happened.

entry.title: anarchic illustrations
entry.summary: Drawings, prints and stickers promoting the abolition of traditional forms of government and institutions. All my anarchic themed illustrations are available as high-res downloads, available under CC by 4.

entry.title: Sauerkraut Recipe
entry.summary: Sauerkraut recipe for Week 26, 2023. Ingredients 2453g cabbage 674g carrot 252g apple 450g celeriac (forgot to weigh it, so this is a guess) 1 packet of sauerkraut seasoning 0.

entry.title: Ghost Work
entry.summary: “A startling exposé of the invisible human workforce that powers the web–and how to bring it out of the shadows. Hidden beneath the surface of the internet, a new, stark reality is looming–one that cuts to the very heart of our endless debates about the impact of AI.

entry.title: Erlaubnis Project Diagrams
entry.summary: These diagrams can also be found on Github. --- title: Erlaubnis Authorization State Diagram --- stateDiagram-v2 [*] -- Request Request -- Verifing: Exchange Client Params Verifing -- Waiting_Approval: Grant Valid Verifing -- Unauthorised: Grant Invalid Waiting_Approval -- Authorised: Grant Approved Waiting_Approval -- Unauthorised: Grant Denied Waiting_Approval -- Timed_Out: Grant Unserviced Authorised -- Revoked: Grant Revoked Revoked -- [*] Timed_Out -- [*] Unauthorised -- [*] Authorised -- [*]--- title: Erlaubnis Entity Relationship Diagram --- erDiagram USER { string id string username string password datetime created_at datetime updated_at } ROLE { integer id string name string description } CLIENT { string id string client_name string token_endpoint_auth_method array grant_types } METADATA { string logo_uri array contacts string policy_uri string tos_uri string client_uri } CONFIGURATION { integer version string jwks string jwks_uri string scope } REGISTRATION_RECORD { int id foreign_key user_id foreign_key client_id datetime installation_time } INSTALLATION_RECORD { int id foreign_key user_id foreign_key client_id foreign_key configuration datetime installation_time } STATE_RECORD { foreign_key user_id foreign_key client string state } USER ||--o{ ROLE : has USER ||--o{ CLIENT : registers REGISTRATION_RECORD ||--o{ CLIENT : generates INSTALLATION_RECORD ||--o{ CLIENT : generates STATE_RECORD ||--o{ CLIENT : generates USER ||--o{ CLIENT : installs CLIENT ||--o{ CONFIGURATION : contains CLIENT ||--||METADATA : contains

entry.title: neocolonialism
entry.summary: Intrusion of foreign economic domination, it can include military and economic intervention in the form of structural adjustment programs. Neocolonialism is seem in states that have already achieved independence from colonial rule.

entry.title: structural adjustment programs
entry.summary: Structural adjustment programs are a [[notes/neocolonialism|neocolonist]] tool that sets out a set of economic reforms that a country must adhere to secure a loan from the International Monetary Fund and/or the World Bank.

entry.title: cash crops
entry.summary: A crop that is grown to sell for profit rather than for the benefit of the farmer. Used to distinguish between marketed crops from subsistence crops in subsistence agriculture.

entry.title: Expect Resistance: A Crimethink Field Manual
entry.summary: Expect Resistance is not one but three books, each of which may be read as a complete work unto itself. The first book, printed in standard black ink, continues the inquiry into modern life and its discontents begun in Days of War, Nights of Love.

entry.title: Consensus and Humming in the IETF
entry.summary: This post summarises the key points of RFC 7282 an informational RFC (Request for Comment) published in 2014. This post is?

entry.title: E2E Fear Campaign -- ACCCE & Police
entry.summary: Post on Twitter Post on Mastodon 1/14 The ACCCE & police are currently running a fear campaign about end to end encryption centering their arguments this time on concerns about sextortion of teenagers.

entry.title: Embodiment
entry.summary: The principles of embodiment look at diverse experiences and relationships as human beings embodying multiple identities and realities in “disembodied” online spaces.

entry.title: Feminist Principles of the Internet
entry.summary: The Feminist Principles of the Internet are a series of statements that offer a gender and sexual rights lens on critical Internet-related rights.

entry.title: Freedom is not Given its Taken
surveillance capitalism
entry.summary: Surveillance capitalism is a type of capitalism market form, and a [[technocratic]]-oriented phenomenon that centres on the commodification of personal data by corporations.

entry.title: technocratic
entry.summary: Technocracy is a form of government in which the decision-makers are selected based on their expertise in a given area of responsibility, particularly with regard to scientific or technical knowledge.

entry.title: The Age of Surveillance Capitalism
entry.summary: The challenges to humanity posed by the digital future, the first detailed examination of the unprecedented form of power called ‘surveillance capitalism,’ and the quest by powerful corporations to predict and control us.

entry.title: the state
entry.summary: The “state” is a centralised political organisation that imposes and enforces rules over a population within a territory. [!warning]

entry.title: Work in Progress
entry.summary: Work that is still in progress and being worked on. Freedom is not Given its TakenCheck it out on Ko-Fi →

entry.title: Autobase
entry.summary: An experimental module that’s used to rebase (follow?) multiple casually linked [[hypercore|Hypercore]] into a single, linearized Hypercore for multi-user collaboration.

entry.title: BTree
entry.summary: A self-balancing tree data structure that is used to store data in a sorted and searchable manner.

entry.title: DHT
entry.summary: A decentralized data structure that is used to store and retrieve data over a network. The HyperDHT is used to facilitate the discovery of peers and to enable connections between them.

entry.title: DID
entry.summary: Decentralized identifier (DIDs) identifies any subject (e.g., a person, organization, thing, data model, abstract entity, etc.) that the controller of the DID decides that it identifies.

entry.title: Holepunch
entry.summary: Holepunch is a company and suite of tools that “equips developers with a powerful suite of independent components to effortlessly construct peer-to-peer applications.

entry.title: Hyperbee
entry.summary: An append-only [[b-tree|B-Tree]] running on Hypercore. It provides a key-value store API with mechanisms that can be used for: - Inserting and retrieving key-value pairs; - Atomic batch insertion; - Creation of sorted iterators.

entry.title: Hypercore
entry.summary: A distributed, peer to peer, append-only log that can be used to create fast scalable applications without a backend.

entry.title: HyperDHT
entry.summary: A [[dht|DHT]] powering [[Hyperswarm]], each server is bound to a unique key pair, where the client connects to the server using the servers public key.

entry.title: Hyperdrive
entry.summary: A real-time distributed file system.

entry.title: Hyperswarm
entry.summary: A high-level API for finding and connecting to peers who are interested in a “topic”.

entry.title: IPFS
entry.summary: InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) is a protocol, hypermedia and file sharing peer-to-peer network for storing and sharing data to a distributed file system.

entry.title: Setting Up Distributed Press on Digital Ocean
entry.summary: [!info] Environment Metadata Ubuntu 22.10 x64 Distributed Press v1.2.1 About Distributed Press Distributed Press is an open source publishing tool for the World Wide Web and Distributed Web.

entry.title: The Inner Sanctum
entry.summary: The Inner Sanctum (2022), gouache, watercolor, and ink on paper, 100 x 70 centimeters, Colossal

entry.title: The Offering
entry.summary: The Offering, gouache painting by Bill Mayer, Bill Mayer’s Personal Website

entry.title: Vegetable Portraits
entry.summary: Portraits of my favourite and seasonal vegetables created using a variety of artistic mediums, they act as consistent theme that records changes in art style.

entry.title: reproductive labor
entry.summary: Reproductive labor was first brought into the discourse during the wages for housework campaign during the 1970s (discussed in Revolution at Point Zero).

entry.title: Aquarium Algae Scrubber
entry.summary: An algae scrubber is a water filtering device thats used to grow algae, which in the process, filters undesirable chemicals like nitrate, phosphate, nitrite, ammonia, ammonium from the water.

entry.title: Ars moriendi
entry.summary: Ars moriendi, woodblock 7 of 11, Circa 1460, illustrator unknown, en wikipedia. Source The Ars Moriendi (The Art of Dying) is a Mediaeval Christian death manual that appeared around the middle of the fifteenth century.

entry.title: Coaster for Salut
entry.summary: Coaster for Salute, gouache painting by @rowan_sugar, Coaster 3/6 for the Salut!, 2023

entry.title: Lago d'Averno
entry.summary: Lago d’Averno, gouache painting by Jacob Philipp Hackert, 1794.

entry.title: The history of witches and wizards
entry.summary: The history of witches and wizards: giving a true account of all their tryals in England, Scotland, Swedeland, France, and New England; with their confession and condemnation, circa 1720, Collected from Bishop Hall, Bishop Morton, Sir Matthew Hale, etc.

entry.title: Theatrum Chemicum Britannicum
entry.summary: Theatrum Chemicum Britannicum, woodblock, circa 1652, Elias Ashmole, The Public Domain Review

entry.title: Witches dancing in a circle with the devil
entry.summary: The history of witches and wizards: giving a true account of all their tryals in England, Scotland, Swedeland, France, and New England; with their confession and condemnation, circa 1720, Collected from Bishop Hall, Bishop Morton, Sir Matthew Hale, etc.

entry.title: Witches flying on broomsticks
entry.summary: The history of witches and wizards: giving a true account of all their tryals in England, Scotland, Swedeland, France, and New England; with their confession and condemnation, circa 1720, Collected from Bishop Hall, Bishop Morton, Sir Matthew Hale, etc.

entry.title: woodblock
entry.summary: A technique originally developed in China as a way of printing on textiles and later on paper, the oldest surviving fragments to survive date back to the Han dynasty (before 220).

entry.title: 100 days of gouache
entry.summary: The concept of a 100 days project is you commit to doing something consistently for 100 days straight. For a non sequential 100 days I created a [[notes/gouache|gouache]] painting using 100 Days of Drawing by Jennifer Lewis.

entry.title: gouache
entry.summary: Gouache is a water based medium made of pigment, water and a binding agent, normally gum arabic or dextrin. Gouache is similar to watercolour in that it can be re-hydrated and it infuses with the paper.

entry.title: The Feminist and the Sex Offender
entry.summary: In the era of Me Too and mass incarceration, The Feminist and the Sex Offender makes a powerful feminist case for accountability without punishment and sexual safety and pleasure without injustice.

entry.title: the art studio
entry.summary: If you’ve been enjoying my work and finding it entertaining. Your support means the world to me, and I can’t thank you enough for being a part of this journey.

entry.title: Anarchic Agreements, a Field Guide to Collective Organising
entry.summary: A new world is possible and not just in our hearts. Anarchic Agreements is a quintessential field guide for the revolution, answering the practical questions often left out of works of political theory and philosophy.

entry.title: the antilibrary
entry.summary: a scholar conscious of the power of [their] antilibrary is not concerned with treating knowledge as a property to possess or consume; rather […] how much you don’t know—and how to find out that information when you need it.

entry.title: errbufferoverfl
entry.summary: The easiest way to pronounce it is “error buffer overflow”… like the security vulnerability.

entry.title: Wadawurrung and Dja Dja Wurrung People
entry.summary: Wadawurrung Country Wadawurrung country encompasses the area from the Great Dividing Range of Ballarat, the coast from the Werribee River to Mangowak (Airey’s Inlet), including Djilang (Geelong), Ballaarat (Ballarat), Torquay, Jan Jook, the Bellarine Peninsula and Surf Coast.

entry.title: Lost in Work Escaping Capitalism
entry.summary: ‘Work hard, get paid.’ It’s simple. Self-evident. But it’s also a lie—at least for most of us. For young people today, the old assumptions are crumbling; hard work in school no longer guarantees a secure, well-paying job in the future.

entry.title: Revolution at Point Zero
entry.summary: Written between 1974 and 2016, Revolution at Point Zero collects four decades of research and theorising on the nature of housework, social reproduction, and women’s struggles on this terrain—to escape it, to better its conditions, to reconstruct it in ways that provide an alternative to capitalist relations.

entry.title: Collapsology: Why your biggest threat isn’t exposed RDP
entry.summary: In September 2021, I presented a conference talk entitled “Collapsology: Why Exposed RDP Isn’t Your Biggest Threat” where I gave an overview of how to identify cultural threats in a rapidly evolving business landscape, and how we can use modern-day tools to identify these threats before they result in security culture collapse.

entry.title: The Big Cloud Security Debate: Hackers vs Security Engineers
entry.summary: A debate organised by ANZ, Contino and Meetup Madness where 4 hackers vs 4 security experts engaging in a fiery debate to determine all the ways they can hack and defend cloud environments.

entry.title: A Brief History of Tamper Evident Locks
entry.summary: A talk that discussed common “canary locks” or locks with tamper evident mechanisms through the ages, Buffy trawled through 11,335,427 patents to identify several high and low profile locks and their known, or speculated bypass techniques.

entry.title: Snake Charming for Beginners
entry.summary: Snake-charming is an age-old practice of hypnotizing snakes by playing and waving a murli - in the modern day this practice looks much different, equipped with an Integrated Development Environment (IDE), a clackity keyboard and a trusty guide we’ll be taking you through how you can effectively charm Python 3.

entry.title: Linux Users Victoria Main Meeting Kali Linux
entry.summary: Come together in ritual to learn about KALI LINUX! A Debian based Penetration Testing and Ethical Hacking distribution, with over 600 pre-installed packages.

entry.title: Agloe: What the Map Makers of the 1930s can Teach us About Protecting our Data in 2018
entry.summary: What does the little town of Agloe, Colchester, NY have in common with modern day data protection? Why when I look for directions to Agloe, Colchester, NY do I only get a partial match?

entry.title: Building your own offline file-sharing and communication system for the inevitable downfall of the Internet
entry.summary: Have you ever considered a world where the Internet in all it’s grandeur and tyre-fire-ness has finally imploded. Well after living for 8 months without the Internet I did and it looked a little like this.
